canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, metaphor detection is defined as the observable task of identifying whether a sentence contains a metaphor. This behavior is operationalized as a prediction task where a model assigns a corresponding label to a given sentence. The available sources situate this task within the broader study of figurative language understanding, where it is treated as a form of figurative speech. It is frequently studied alongside other complex linguistic phenomena, such as sarcasm and hyperbole. One paper also groups the detection of metaphors with the detection of harmful language patterns like sexism.
papers: DeAL: Decoding-time Alignment for Large Language Models
How to Enable Effective Cooperation Between Humans andNLPModels: A Survey of Principles, Formalizations, and Beyond

definitions.text: The observable task of identifying whether a given sentence contains a metaphor, typically by assigning a corresponding label.
definitions.paper: How to Enable Effective Cooperation Between Humans andNLPModels: A Survey of Principles, Formalizations, and Beyond

relationships.evidence: We concentrate on three tasks for our analyses and experiments: two types of figurative language (sarcasm and metaphors) and one form of deviant language (sexism)...
